{"file_path": "d:\CapsoneProject\Mobile\green-lens-app\.cursor\rules\00-core-project.mdc", "content": "---
description: Core non-negotiable GreenLens standards
alwaysApply: true
---

# GreenLens Core Rules

- Place files in the correct layer. `app/` is only for Expo Router screens/layouts.
- Use design tokens (`bg-primary`, `text-primary`) instead of hard-coded hex in className.
- Every `Pressable` must have press animation using `react-native-reanimated`.
- Every fetched list needs a skeleton loader; do not use only spinner for list loading.
- Do not use React Native legacy `Animated` API; use Reanimated.
- Do not add new libraries without a clear reason.
- Every component props shape must use a TypeScript interface; never use `any`.
"}

{"file_path": "d:\CapsoneProject\Mobile\green-lens-app\.cursor\rules\00-core-project.mdc", "content": "---
description: Core non-negotiable GreenLens standards
alwaysApply: true
---

# GreenLens Core Rules

- Place files in the correct layer. `app/` is only for Expo Router screens/layouts.
- Use design tokens (`bg-primary`, `text-primary`) instead of hard-coded hex in className.
- Every `Pressable` must have press animation using `react-native-reanimated`.
- Every fetched list needs a skeleton loader; do not use only spinner for list loading.
- Do not use React Native legacy `Animated` API; use Reanimated.
- Do not add new libraries without a clear reason.
- Every component props shape must use a TypeScript interface; never use `any`.
"}

{"file_path": "d:\CapsoneProject\Mobile\green-lens-app\.cursor\rules\08-natural-intent-routing.mdc", "content": "---
description: Natural language routing for GreenLens agents and commands
alwaysApply: true
---

# Natural Intent Routing

When the user writes natural Vietnamese/English requests, infer intent and route automatically without requiring exact command syntax.

- If request is about crashes, runtime/build errors, Metro, NativeWind, Router, Reanimated, use `rn-debugger`.
- If request asks to create or refine a screen/page in `app/`, use `screen-builder`.
- If request asks to create or refine a reusable UI component, use `ui-component-builder`.
- If request is about integrating backend endpoints, types, or React Query hooks, use `api-integrator`.
- If request asks for code review, risk check, convention compliance, or regression checks, use `code-reviewer`.
- If request asks for research/docs comparison/best-practice decision, use `researcher`.
- If request asks for implementation planning phases/roadmap, follow `.cursor/commands/plan.md` and use `planner` + `plan-reviewer` when needed.
- If request asks to implement from an existing plan, follow `.cursor/commands/execute.md`.

Routing behavior:

- Prefer automatic routing from meaning, not exact keywords.
- Accept synonyms and casual phrases (e.g., "lÃ m mÃ n login", "app bá»‹ vÄƒng", "review giÃºp", "ná»‘i API", "lÃªn plan").
- If intent is ambiguous between two routes, ask one short clarification question, then proceed.
- If task is simple and does not need a specialist, proceed directly without forcing a subagent.
- Keep responses concise and action-first.
"}

{"file_path": "d:\CapsoneProject\Mobile\green-lens-app\.cursor\commands\README.md", "content": "# Cursor Commands (Project)

These command prompts are mirrored from `.claude/commands`.

Available files:
- coding-level.md
- code-review.md
- consume-api.md
- execute.md
- fix.md
- learn.md
- plan.md

How to use in Cursor:
- Open a command file and paste/adapt its prompt into chat.
- Or reference it directly in chat, for example: "Run prompt from `.cursor/commands/plan.md`".

Natural phrases also work (auto-routing by intent):
- "lÃªn plan tÃ­nh nÄƒng nÃ y" -> `plan.md`
- "implement theo plan nÃ y" -> `execute.md`
- "review code giÃºp mÃ¬nh" -> `code-review.md`
- "app Ä‘ang lá»—i/crash" -> `fix.md`
"}

{"file_path": "d:\CapsoneProject\Mobile\green-lens-app\.cursor\agents\README.md", "content": "# Cursor Agents (Project)

These agent definitions are mirrored from `.claude/agents` and optimized for Cursor models to reduce token cost.

## Model policy

- Default: `composer-2-fast` (cheap/fast) for implementation and routine tasks.
- Complex reasoning only: `gpt-5.5-medium` for `planner`, `plan-reviewer`, `code-reviewer`, `rn-debugger`.

## Available agents

- api-integrator
- code-reviewer
- docs-manager
- git-manager
- planner
- plan-reviewer
- project-manager
- researcher
- rn-debugger
- screen-builder
- scout
- ui-component-builder

Usage:
- Ask Cursor to follow a definition by file path in `.cursor/agents/`.
- If needed, we can force stricter routing via hook matcher rules.

Natural routing examples:
- "app bá»‹ crash khi má»Ÿ map" -> `rn-debugger`
- "táº¡o mÃ n report detail" -> `screen-builder`
- "lÃ m component card má»›i" -> `ui-component-builder`
- "ná»‘i API report list" -> `api-integrator`
- "review convention giÃºp" -> `code-reviewer`
- "so sÃ¡nh giáº£i phÃ¡p trÆ°á»›c khi code" -> `researcher`
"}

{"file_path": "d:\CapsoneProject\Mobile\green-lens-app\.cursor\rules\09-task-suggestion-selector.mdc", "content": "---
description: Show suggested project tasks for user selection before execution
alwaysApply: true
---

# Task Suggestion Selector

Before executing substantial work, provide a short "Suggested actions" list and let the user select.

Selection behavior:

- Infer top intent from natural language.
- Suggest 3-5 options max, ordered by relevance.
- Include:
  - Primary agent suggestion
  - Optional command suggestion
  - Optional related rules that will be applied
- Ask for a selection when intent is ambiguous or when multiple valid routes exist.
- If intent is very clear and user explicitly asks to proceed, execute directly.

Output format:

- `Detected intent: <short text>`
- `Suggested actions:`
  - `[1] Agent: <name> - <why>`
  - `[2] Command: <name> - <why>`
  - `[3] Direct execution - <what will be done>`
- `Reply with 1/2/3 (or custom).`

Routing hints:

- Crash/build/runtime issues -> `rn-debugger`
- Create screen in `app/` -> `screen-builder`
- Create/refine UI component -> `ui-component-builder`
- API/types/query integration -> `api-integrator`
- Code review/convention check -> `code-reviewer`
- Research/compare solutions -> `researcher`
- Plan roadmap/phases -> command `plan` + `planner`/`plan-reviewer`
- Execute existing plan -> command `execute`
"}

{"file_path": "d:\CapsoneProject\Mobile\green-lens-app\.cursor\commands\task-selector.md", "content": "---
name: task-selector
description: Suggest and select the best agent/command/rules before execution
---

Analyze `$ARGUMENTS` and propose the best execution route for this project.

Requirements:

1. Detect intent from natural language.
2. Suggest up to 5 actions:
   - Best agent
   - Best command
   - Direct execution option
   - Optional backup route
3. Explain each suggestion in one short line.
4. Ask the user to select one option (number or custom instruction).
5. After user selection, proceed with that route.

Output template:

Detected intent: <intent>

Suggested actions:
- [1] Agent: <agent-name> - <reason>
- [2] Command: <command-name> - <reason>
- [3] Direct execution - <reason>
- [4] Backup route - <reason>

Reply with 1/2/3/4 (or custom).
"}
